strict_win_deltas.delta: P2 adds a dedicated `parseVersionFormat(argv)` function mirroring the existing `parseNameFlag` helper already in bin/cli.js, while P1 inlines the `formatIndex`/`format` parsing logic directly inside the `case 'version':` block, mixing parsing and output concerns.

P1's invalid-format test asserts `error.status === 1 && /Unsupported format: yaml/.test(error.stderr)`, while P2's equivalent test only does `assert.throws(() => run(...))` with no check on exit code or stderr, so it wouldn't catch a future regression that changed the exit status away from 1.

P2's JSON test pins the exact single-line output contract via `assert.match(out, /^\{"version":"\d+\.\d+\.\d+"\}\n$/)`, matching the spec's literal `{"version":"<x.y.z>"}` (single line) requirement, whereas P1's test only calls `JSON.parse(out)` and regex-checks `parsed.version`, not the full output shape.

P2's `parseVersionFormat` returns a normalized `'plain'|'json'` value that future format additions can extend without touching the `case 'version':` block, while P1's ad hoc `formatIndex`/ternary logic inline in the switch-case is harder to extend or reuse.
